hook_text: The call we get every March from companies who ignored our October demo.
body:
October: "We're happy with our current process. We'll revisit in Q2."

March: "We just got a Sanad violation notice. Can you help?"

It happens every year.

March in Oman: end of Q1. Companies that haven't done Sanad compliance reviews since year-end are hit by the quarterly audit cycle.

The violation pattern is consistent:
- Profession code mismatches built up over the year
- Worker transfers not formally documented
- Visa expiry warnings ignored (no automated alerts)
- Omanization ratios drifted without anyone noticing

Most of these are fixable before March — if someone's looking.

Nobody was looking because nobody had a system that looked for them.

SmartPRO Hub runs continuous Sanad compliance monitoring. It flags issues the week they appear, not the quarter the inspector arrives.

Our March support tickets from existing clients: typically 2-3 per year, minor issues.
From non-clients who call us in crisis: 8-12 in March alone.

---

What does your Sanad monitoring process look like between audit cycles?
cta: Comment "SANAD" for our compliance monitoring guide.
rejected_reason: pre-PR#12 backlog purge — queue hygiene 2026-07-15 (regenerate via corrected pipeline)
